chapter_context.summary: Pronounces a woe on greedy landowners who plot evil on their beds and seize fields by violence, rebukes the false prophets who tell the true prophets not to prophesy, and concludes with a sudden and beautiful promise of gathering the remnant of Israel like sheep in a fold.

outline.text: Woe to Greedy Landowners: Oppressing Men of Their Inheritance and Facing Divine Recompense
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Woe to Greedy Landowners: Oppressing Men of Their Inheritance and Facing Divine Recompense (2:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Micah.

outline.text: The Conflict of Prophecy: Rebuking False Prophets who Encourage Complacency and Luxury
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Conflict of Prophecy: Rebuking False Prophets who Encourage Complacency and Luxury (2:6-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Micah.

outline.text: Remnant Promise of Gathering: The Remnant of Jacob gathered as Sheep in Bozrah and Their King Passing Before Them
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Remnant Promise of Gathering: The Remnant of Jacob gathered as Sheep in Bozrah and Their King Passing Before Them (2:12-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Micah.

key_themes.title: True Worship as Ethical Integrity
key_themes.description: Micah clarifies that religious rituals are meaningless without ethical behavior in everyday commercial, legal, and social interactions.

key_themes.title: Messianic Hope and Bethlehem
key_themes.description: The book introduces the eternal ruler coming from Bethlehem, pointing to the ultimate messianic restoration and security of God's sheep.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and requirements in Chapter 2, what does it practically mean for you to walk humbly with God in your current season of life?
How does the final vision of a compassionate God casting our sins into the deep sea inspire your daily worship and forgiveness of others?

cultural_historical.title: The Southern Kingdom under Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah
cultural_historical.text: Micah's ministry spanned the reigns of three kings of Judah, representing a period of political tension with Assyria, extreme corruption of the elite classes in Jerusalem, and eventually the reforming reign of Hezekiah.
